coverage.name: Bloom Energy
coverage.thesis: **Day's #1 watchlist long (+3.3%) and we own it both books.** Live 3 sh @ $222.64, paper 40 sh @ $218.90; $188 GTC stop confirmed on each. Catalyst is real: the $1.7B Oaktree/IDF financing to deploy Bloom fuel cells for AI data-center power, with Q2 earnings 7/28 - a power-demand theme decoupled from the chip-capex fear crushing the rest of the tape, which is why BE was green on a -1.8% Nasdaq day. But it's a battleground: BE publicly rebutted the Hunterbrook short report ('false and misleading') and it faded from its $223.6 high into a risk-off close, still far under its wrecked 20/50-day. A catalyst bounce on a wide stop, not a trend - so we hold into the weekend, don't add, and keep the $201 dry.
coverage.hold_reason: We own BE because it's the day's clear catalyst leader - up ~3% on a real $1.7B financing to power AI data centers with its fuel cells, running into 7/28 earnings. Our live entry ($222.64) was late so we caught little of the pop, but the multi-day swing thesis is intact. What we're watching: the Hunterbrook short allegations (BE rebutted them, but a follow-up is the weekend gap risk the $188 stop can't fully cover) - so we hold modest rather than add.
coverage.size_note: Own both books; $188 stop each. Held into the weekend - not adding into the short-battleground.

projection.basis: oversold catalyst bounce; $1.7B Nebius AI-power deal, contested by short

coverage.name: ProShares UltraPro Short QQQ
coverage.thesis: **Our trend-aligned inverse and today's top pick (pop_rank 1).** Hold 210 @ $42.54 (paper, ~10%) with a wide $38 GTC stop. QQQ $695 is deeply under its 20- and 50-day and rolling on China's Kimi K3 'AI shock' into a risk-off weekend, so the lab leans short. We didn't add into the intraday fade (SQQQ already +4.7% off its low) - instead we cut the offside chip weight (NVDA trim) so the standing SQQQ carries a bigger share of a lighter book. The $38 stop exits if QQQ reclaims and the flush is over.
coverage.hold_reason: We hold SQQQ because the leverage-long regime is broken (QQQ under its 20- and 50-day) and on down days the only green on our watchlist is the inverse. It's deliberately a starter with a wide stop: if Monday gaps up on a 'buy-the-reset' bounce, the $38 stop caps it; if the flush continues, we own some of the green. We chose to trim longs rather than pile more into the inverse after its intraday run.
coverage.size_note: Held starter; $38 GTC stop. Carries a bigger share of a lighter book after the NVDA trim.

projection.basis: hedge pops if chip derate extends into hyperscaler earnings

coverage.name: Direxion Daily Semi Bear 3x
coverage.thesis: **The day's single best watchlist mover (+4.8%, peaked +5.9%) - the sharpest expression of the chip rout, and we did NOT own it.** SOXS is the 3x-inverse of the semis leading the whole tape lower (SOXX gauge -20% from its record on AI-capex fears). We hold the milder SQQQ (3x-inverse Nasdaq) instead, so we captured the direction but not the sharpest amplitude - that gap is the honest miss on a down day. Extended after a multi-day run, so chasing it into the Friday close would have been the low-quality entry we avoided; it's a Monday-breakdown re-entry candidate, not a chase.
coverage.hold_reason: We don't own SOXS - we track it as the sharpest inverse and today's best mover. If Monday confirms more downside (QQQ can't reclaim ~$718, semis break again), SOXS is the highest-amplitude way to own the green; if the tape bounces off oversold it fades fast, so it's an on-confirmation trade only, not a hold-through-a-bounce.
coverage.size_note: Not held; sharp-inverse re-entry candidate on a fresh Monday breakdown.

projection.basis: 3x inverse semis; same Moonshot/Iran risk-off, higher beta

coverage.name: Rocket Lab
coverage.thesis: **Second-strongest mover (+3.5%) - space/launch reclaiming hard off Wednesday's flush.** Snapped back from the -11.6% analyst-downgrade dip; the $8B Iridium deal + NASA wins are intact, targets $110-130. We ADDED in paper this run (70 -> 130 sh @ ~$69.7) with the UPRO proceeds - rotating dead 3x-index beta into a confirmed single-name mover. Stop raised to $62 (below today's $64.5 low).
coverage.hold_reason: RKLB fell ~12% Wednesday on one analyst going Neutral on its pending $8B Iridium deal, but the deal and its NASA launch wins are intact and targets sit $110-130. We added to it in paper today - taking the money freed from cutting UPRO and concentrating it into the day's #2 mover as it reclaims toward the $74-80 gap it fell from. The $62 stop, just under today's low, is the line; we ride the reclaim and sell into the gap-fill.
coverage.size_note: Added 60 sh from the UPRO cut; stop $62, sell into $74-80.

projection.basis: oversold space name; snaps back on any risk-on Monday

coverage.hold_reason: Held in the paper lab (16 sh @ $260.68, stop $236). Nuclear/energy-for-AI name showing relative strength - green while the complex whipsawed. We hold it as steady RS ballast while it leads; the $236 stop is the floor.

coverage.name: SoFi
coverage.thesis: **Fintech, +1.1% - drifting up with a mixed tape, no catalyst.** We exited it live on 7/15; no reason to re-own without a catalyst. Watch.

coverage.name: NVIDIA
coverage.thesis: **Trimmed the single biggest drag 130->70 (sold 60 @ ~$202) into the afternoon bounce.** NVDA is the epicenter of a chip-led rout - China's Moonshot Kimi K3 'AI shock', chips' worst month since 2008 (SOXX -18% in July), and Apple just passed it as the world's most valuable company. It bounced off $198 (held the $190s shelf) but sits under its 50-day in a confirmed-broken regime, so we cut the ~29%-of-book concentration to ~16% - selling into strength, not the low - and kept the $186 GTC stop on the remaining 70. A weight cut to fit the regime, not a thesis-kill.
coverage.hold_reason: We still hold 70 NVDA because it held its $190s support shelf and is the highest-quality chip name for when the tape turns - but it was ~29% of the book and the biggest bleeder in a chip-specific rout, so we halved the weight into the bounce rather than camp it. What we're watching: a QQQ reclaim of its 20-day (~$718) to re-add, or a break of the $190s to exit the rest on the stop.
coverage.size_note: Trimmed 130->70 into the bounce; $186 GTC stop on the rest.

projection.basis: bellwether snapback vs Moonshot overhang; hyperscaler earnings the tell

coverage.name: Palantir
coverage.thesis: **AI-software laggard, flat (-0.1%), no fresh catalyst - bleeding sideways with the tape.** Retired its live reclaim ticket earlier (never hit the $134.8 trigger). Paper holds 50 sh @ $135.22 (stop $121.5); if $129-130 breaks we rotate the capital into a real catalyst.
coverage.hold_reason: Palantir is a high-beta software name with no fresh catalyst - drifting flat with the tape. We hold 50 sh in paper (stop $121.50); it's a laggard, and if the $129-130 shelf breaks we rotate that capital into a name with an actual catalyst. For now it's holding the shelf, so the stop does the work.

coverage.name: Tesla
coverage.thesis: **Mega-cap high-beta, -2.1% - among the weaker large-caps today, fading with the soft tape.** No fresh company catalyst; it's tracking the risk-off derate, not leading. No position; watch for a reclaim or a catalyst.
coverage.size_note: No position; red with the tape, no catalyst - watch.

coverage.name: 3x Semis (Direxion)
coverage.thesis: **Cut today - rotated out of the fragile 3x-semi long.** We sold the paper 80 sh @ ~$136.1 and moved the proceeds into the SQQQ inverse. SOXL is 3x daily semiconductors: in a confirmed-broken regime (QQQ under its 20/50-day) and the chips' worst month since 2008, holding a 3x-long-semi is exactly the decay/whipsaw risk the week's #1 lesson says to shed. It ripped $116->$144 intraday off the open capitulation, then faded - classic momentum-unwind chop. Back on the watchlist as a re-entry candidate only when semis base and QQQ reclaims its 20-day.
coverage.hold_reason: We no longer hold SOXL - cut it this afternoon. It's the most fragile leverage on the book in a broken regime, and it was fading toward its $133 stop into a risk-off weekend, so we took the ~-1.9% exit at $136 rather than wait for the stop and freed the capital for the trend-aligned SQQQ inverse. We'd look at SOXL again only on a real semi base + a QQQ 20-day reclaim.

projection.basis: most fragile 3x long in a confirmed-broken semi regime

coverage.name: 3x S&P (ProShares)
coverage.thesis: **3x S&P, -1.4% - we EXITED it in paper this run.** It was dead-weight leverage in a soft tape with QQQ under its 20-day; per the standing adjust we cut it and rotated the ~$13k into RKLB plus a weekend cash buffer. No leveraged-index long until QQQ reclaims its 20-day.
coverage.size_note: Exited paper today; no 3x-index long until the regime heals.

coverage.name: 3x Nasdaq (ProShares)
coverage.thesis: **3x-Nasdaq - stopped out live at $66.38 this morning; bounced off its own low but still -2.7%.** Only re-arms as the live core when QQQ reclaims its 20-day (~$719). No re-lever until the regime flips green.
coverage.size_note: Stopped live; no re-entry until QQQ reclaims ~$719.

projection.basis: 3x Nasdaq snapback beta - but regime-gated out until QQQ reclaims $718

coverage.name: 3x FANG+ (MicroSectors)
coverage.thesis: **3x FANG+ - stopped out live at $25.29 near the low today; weakest of the 3x complex (-4.3%).** NFLX / Alphabet-Gemini overhang are fresh FANG drags. Stay out until FANG stabilizes and the regime turns.
coverage.size_note: Stopped live; 3x FANG, avoid until regime heals.

feed.text: 8:10pm - after-hours is closed now, so the book is locked till Monday's 9:30 open. One more broker re-verify: all 7 positions GTC-stopped (live BE $188; paper BE/CEG/NVDA/PLTR/RKLB/SQQQ), zero naked, queue clean, no open tickets. Nothing dislocated after the bell - BE ~$215, the SQQQ hedge closed green (+5.2%). Grade stays final C-; Monday is the QQQ-vs-$718 fork, nothing pre-armed blind over the weekend.

7:40pm weekend re-verify - independently re-pulled both books off the broker: all 7 positions still GTC-stopped (live BE $188; paper BE $188/CEG $236/NVDA $186/PLTR $121.5/RKLB $62/SQQQ $38), zero naked, queue clean, no open tickets. After-hours confirmed Friday's close (QQQ -3.2% under its $718 20-day; BE +4.0%; inverses the only green) - nothing dislocated, grade stays final C-.

7:13pm weekend re-verify - independently re-pulled both books off the broker: all 7 positions GTC-stopped (live BE $188; paper BE/CEG/NVDA/PLTR/RKLB/SQQQ), zero naked, queue clean, no open tickets. Confirmed the weekend catalysts unchanged: BE's $1.7B Nebius fuel-cell deal is real but contested, and the chip derate is a Moonshot-AI/Iran risk-off (SPY only -1%), not a broad breakdown. Holding the QQQ-$718 fork into Monday; no blind weekend pre-arm.

6:41pm weekend re-verify - independently re-pulled both books off the broker: live BE (3 sh, $188 GTC stop) + $201 cash, paper's 6 names (BE/CEG/NVDA/PLTR/RKLB/SQQQ) all GTC-stopped, zero naked, queue clean, no active tickets. No after-hours dislocation: QQQ closed $695 (still ~3% under its 20-day), BE steady ~$214.5 on no fresh news. Holding the de-risked, hedged book into the weekend - Monday's open is the QQQ-vs-$718 fork; no blind pre-arm.

6:13pm Friday weekly consolidation - folded the week (7/14-7/17) into the Playbook and promoted 3 earned rules: cut leveraged-index longs when QQQ is under its 20-day (drove the -14.6% week), a GTC stop can't protect a 3x ETF through an overnight gap (de-lever same-session), and wide-stops/never-naked (held 7/7). Capture trend logged honestly (49%->~0%->~0%->~0%, flat). Both books re-verified off the broker: 7/7 GTC-stopped, zero naked; BE deal intact on no fresh weekend news. Holding into the weekend, no blind pre-arm.

5:10pm weekend re-verify - independently re-pulled both books off the broker: 7/7 positions GTC-stopped (live BE $188; paper BE/CEG/NVDA/PLTR/RKLB/SQQQ), zero naked, queue clean, no active tickets. BE ~$215 on no fresh weekend news (Hunterbrook short-report vs Oaktree/Nebius battleground unchanged); no after-hours dislocation on any holding. Holding the de-risked, hedged book into the weekend - no blind Monday pre-arm.

4:41pm weekend safety check - verified all 7 positions are GTC-stopped at the broker (live BE $188; paper BE/CEG/NVDA/PLTR/RKLB/SQQQ), zero naked, queue clean. No after-hours news or dislocation on the holdings; BE holding ~$214.5. Not pre-arming anything blind over the weekend - Monday's open is the QQQ-vs-$718 fork.

Post-close finalize - graded the day C-. Owned the two green names (paper SQQQ inverse +5.0%, BE +3.6% both books) and de-risked paper, but both books closed red on a 3rd straight down day -> ~0% capture. Live -9.4% ($843.89) was mostly the inherited overnight-3x gap-stops (TQQQ+FNGU) filling below their stops at the open - the 7/16-flagged risk. All 7 stops held, zero naked. No weekend pre-arm; Monday's open is a QQQ-vs-20-day fork.

3:46pm close-prep: reconciled both books off the broker - nothing traded since the 3:20 NVDA trim, zero naked. LIVE = BE 3 sh (stop $188) + $201 dry; PAPER = 6 names all GTC-stopped + the SQQQ hedge. Not forcing a trade into the Friday bell - powder saved for a clean Monday setup. 0/3 live day-trades used this week; PDT budget full.

Cut the paper NVDA long 130->70 into the afternoon bounce - it was ~29% of the book and the single biggest drag in a chip-led rout (Apple just passed Nvidia; SOXX -18% on the month, worst since 2008). Selling into strength, not the low. Freed ~$12k to Monday powder and re-armed the $186 stop on the remaining 70.

Put a starter into SQQQ - the trend-aligned inverse of the broken QQQ gate. The morning bounce is fading into a risk-off Friday close, so the lab leans short with a bounded, wide-stopped probe instead of camping. Funded by cutting SOXL; the book stays net-long quality names.

Cut the paper SOXL 3x-semi long - the most fragile leverage in a confirmed-broken regime, fading toward its $133 stop into the weekend. Rotated the proceeds into the SQQQ inverse. This is the week's #1 lesson (be lighter on 3x when QQQ's under its 20-day) executed in the lab.

2:18pm verify-and-hold: reconciled both books off the broker - nothing traded since 1:51, zero naked. LIVE = BE 3 sh (stop $188) + $201 dry, equity $853 (-8.4% day). PAPER = 6 names, all GTC-stopped, ~$25k dry. BE eased to +5.4% but stays the watchlist's best long; not adding into the short-seller / weekend overhang. QQQ still under its 20-day -> no re-lever. 0/3 live day-trades used.

1:51pm: executed the standing adjust - trimmed the flat 3x drag (sold paper UPRO), concentrated into RKLB the confirmed mover, re-armed its $62 GTC stop. Both books zero-naked; live holds BE + $201 dry into a risk-off weekend. 0/3 live day-trades used.

Cut the paper UPRO 3x-S&P - dead-weight leverage in a broken regime (QQQ still under its 20-day). Rotated the proceeds into RKLB (added to 130 sh, the #2 mover reclaiming toward its $74-80 gap, $62 stop) and kept the rest dry into the weekend.

1:18pm reconcile: Adam approved BE and it filled (live 3 sh @ $222.64, $188 GTC stop confirmed) - we finally own the day's #1 mover. Retired the NVDA breakout ticket (rejected for BP, as designed). Trailed the paper SOXL stop $125->$133 to lock in most of the reversal. Zero naked, both books; pending tickets now clear.
12:52pm check-in: reconciled live + paper off the broker — zero naked, nothing new traded. BE (approve-now) + NVDA (breakout pre-arm) still rest for one tap till 4pm.

feed.detail: rejected - insufficient BP once BE filled
feed.text: Retired the NVDA breakout - the moment BE filled, the mutually-exclusive NVDA ticket correctly rejected for buying power (as designed), and NVDA never cleanly cleared $206.60 anyway. Right call: own the catalyst leader, not two half-bets.

BE filled - Adam approved at 1:10pm and we own the day's #1 mover (+8% on the confirmed $1.7B fuel-cell-for-AI deal). Entry was late ($222 after it ran from $206.73), so small capture, but we're finally IN the leader with the $188 stop confirmed.
Retired the PLTR approve — Adam tapped it at 9:16 but its $134.8 reclaim trigger never hit (PLTR lagged all day, -2.1%), so nothing filled. Aimed the freed cash at the actual leader instead.
FNGU stopped at $25.29 — the open gapped straight through the stop near the low. Bounded the loss but sold the bottom (it's since bounced to ~$26.6). Tuition for carrying 3x into a broken tape.
TQQQ stopped at $66.38 on the gap-down open — de-risked. Then the whole complex V-reversed off the low. The stop did its job; the mistake was upstream (being in 3x here at all).

feed.detail: paper ~82% deployed; dup SOXL add cancelled
feed.text: Paper lab is now ~82% into the bounce leaders — the bleeding NVDA calls got cut and BE/RKLB/SOXL added, all GTC-stopped, zero naked. Cancelled a duplicate SOXL add I'd staged so it wouldn't over-concentrate.

Two live candidates now staged, mutually exclusive (first to fill takes the cash): BE - bold, the day's leader on the $1.7B AI-power deal, but it carries a fresh 7/16 short-seller overhang - and a cleaner NVDA breakout pre-arm ($206.60 buy-stop, 1x not 3x, the RS-bellwether bounce). Both are single names, not 3x-index, so both respect the still-broken QQQ gate.

Live BE buy filled - 3 sh @ $222.64 on the confirmed $1.7B IDF/Oaktree fuel-cell-for-AI deal (Nebius). Bought a touch high near the $223.6 top so we're red on the entry, but BE closed +4% (our best long) and the $188 GTC stop is on. Holding the catalyst into the weekend.
Paper grabbed RKLB on the reclaim - it got hit 12% yesterday on one analyst going Neutral, but the Iridium deal and NASA wins are intact and it's bouncing at the day's highs. Stop $60.
Paper went heavy into BE - the $1.7B AI-power funding is the cleanest catalyst on the board and it's leading a red tape. Stop $188.
Cut the NVDA calls in paper at -38%. NVDA's the epicenter of this chip dump and OTM calls decaying in a broken regime are dead money - moved it to what's actually working.
FNGU hit its $25.30 stop at the open - bought it yesterday, the chip+Netflix dump gapped it right through. That's the stop doing its job.
TQQQ stopped at the open too - held it a week, the broken Nasdaq finally took it out. We're fully out of 3x-index now, which is exactly where the regime says to be.

~12:48pm verify-and-hold: both books reconciled clean vs the broker, zero naked. Live flat/cash $869 (both 3x stops filled at the open); paper's 7 legs (BE/RKLB/SOXL + NVDA/PLTR/CEG/UPRO) all GTC-stopped. Regime still broken (QQQ ~$700 < 20-day $719.70) - holding cash, not chasing the V-bounce; re-lever only on a QQQ 20-day reclaim.
TQQQ $68.50 GTC stop fired at the open - QQQ gapped 3% under its 20-day, so the 3x-Nasdaq core sold at $66.38 (-$99 vs the $76.29 entry). The de-risk the plan called for, executed at the gap-low.
FNGU $25.30 GTC stop fired at $25.29 - the 3x-FANG sleeve, dragged by Netflix -10%, closed for -$25. Adding it 7/16 into a rolling-over regime was the mistake; it is now off the book.

Midday: live book is 100% cash ($869) after both stops. Not chasing the junk-led bounce (BE +6.3%, RKLB +3.4%) with the regime still broken - armed a PLTR $134.8 reclaim buy-stop as the 1x redeploy and kept the rest dry. No new leverage until QQQ reclaims its 20-day (~$719).

TQQQ stop hit at the open - 10 sh out at $66.38 (stop $68.50, gapped clean through). Entry was $76.29, so -$99. It's already bounced to $68.85; the stop caught the low. The regime gate did its job - just at a bad tick.
FNGU stopped at $25.29 (-$25 from the $29.43 entry). I bought it 7/16 at the open into a broken regime - that add was the mistake, owning it plainly.
Paper bought BE 40 sh @ $218.90, stop $188 - the one green leader on the board (+6.5%), power-for-AI, on the right side of the ex-semis rotation. The lab's bounce shot.
Paper added RKLB 70 sh @ $69.54, stop $60 - space beta bouncing off deeply oversold with the tape.
Paper took SOXL 80 sh @ $138.75, stop $125 - semis went +20% off the open low; the lab's leveraged bet that the reset is in. Bounded by the stop, and only in paper.
Live 100% cash ($869) and staying there this run - not re-levering or chasing the bounce into a Friday weekend in the worst chip month since 2008. Redeploy trigger: QQQ reclaims its 20-day (~720).

5:42am pre-market: new trading day, plan set. Broker re-verified - live TQQQ 10 (@ $68.50 GTC) + FNGU 6 (@ $25.30 GTC) stopped, paper legs stopped, zero naked. QQQ $705.94 still under its 20-day ($719.70) & 50-day ($718.73) = leverage regime broken; SPY holds above its MAs (tech-specific, not broad). TSMC capex fear + Netflix -8% + Alphabet Gemini delay keep tech heavy. No pre-market action (extended hours is limit-only, no stop). Open plan: trim TQQQ into any bounce, lean on trimming FNGU, no new leverage until QQQ reclaims ~$720.

Nothing dislocated since the 7:40 check: both books re-pulled off the broker, all 7 positions GTC-stopped (live BE $188; paper BE/CEG/NVDA/PLTR/RKLB/SQQQ), zero naked, queue clean, no open tickets. After-hours re-confirmed the close (no re-grade): QQQ $695.33 sits ~3.2% under its $718 20-day (leverage regime broken, day 3) while SPY is only -1.0% = a tech-specific derate; BE +4.0% on its real-but-contested $1.7B IDF/Oaktree Nebius fuel-cell deal (Fri's $223.6 spike faded to $214.5); inverses SOXS +5.5% / SQQQ +4.6% the only green. Monday = the QQQ-vs-$718 fork: stay LIGHT (hold BE catalyst + SQQQ paper hedge, live $201 dry, no 3x/chip re-adds) while under; on a $718 reclaim on volume, cover/trim SQQQ and rotate freed paper cash + the live $201 into the single strongest CONFIRMED long via a clean same-session ticket. No blind weekend pre-arm. Accountability stays final:true (C-, ~0% capture, 3rd red day).
